title: dolicholobium rheophilum
intro: Dolicholobium rheophilum, also known as the riverbank dolicholobium, is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Asia, particularly in the Philippines and Malaysia, and is found growing in moist, shaded areas near rivers and streams. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, sore throats, and skin infections.
